node.comment: good professor and caring. A lot of work though and it is quite difficult.

Pretty good professor that genuinely cares that students get the most out of his class. Lectures are fairly organized, although he does go off on tangents on some occasions. Labs are easy and purely participation, but good for learning. 2 Projects and 2 Exams. Not nearly as bad of a class as people make it out to be.
Great prof.

Not sure why Professor Koehler's ratings are so low. He teaches very well, is always eager to explain concepts if anyone doesn't understand, and responds quickly to questions on Piazza. The class wasn't as hard as I expected. it's very manageable to get an A since he offers extra credits on projects and exams.

Not sure why Prof. Koehler has low ratings, he's genuinely a good professor. He has clear grading criteria, lots of opportunity to increase your grade, and he's pretty flexible for a lot of it. Even assignment heavy classes, he gives grace periods. Also super accessible and helpful outside of class. If you show you care, he def does as well.

I'm currently in his class and honestly have no idea why his rating is so low. He provides tons of resources to study with. Yeah, the class does require work and self-studying, but I've learned so much I went from just knowing how to write code to actually understanding how it runs at a lower level, which is pretty cool.

He's rude and monotone. Lectures have a lot of wasted time where he's simply not discussing course material. The homework assignments are tedious chores that don't help you learn. Our TAs had nervous breakdowns in every lab section and sometimes we would sit there for 2 hours learning nothing while they fumbled over their words. Exams were ok.
This professor does give extra credit for lecture participation and rewards course evaluations at the end of the semester. He gives great advice for how to succeed in the class.
Please read emails

Professor Koehler is a steal in comparison to some CS instructors, he literally gives videos of the study guides!, you have an ammunition of resources to prepare you well for the exams. Machine assembly is hard as a first for many, so lots of upset kiddos who haven't learned accountability, but you have lots of opportunities to do well and pass

I was scared to take Koehler because of his reviews but he was a great guy and teacher. I can see how he comes off blunt but that's just how he talks. I went to his office hours multiple times and he was very nice and approachable. Yes of course the course material was difficult but doing labs, homeworks, and exam reviews were very helpful.
Worst professor and worst class all in one
You need lots of time to spend on the class. Lectures are long and boring, and labs are sometimes confusing. Prepare to self-study.

One of the most caring professors, he goes above and beyond to ensure you learn. Lectures are well planned (PPTs, videos & notes before class) He provides videos for HW's, projects and study guide. Grades are balanced so your learning efforts are never in vain. You are highly incentivized to keep trying, which genuinely helps you master this class

This professor was rude in emails, vague in answers, and unclear in lectures. He solves problems without explaining, makes grading confusing, and gives projects with unnecessary stories. Overall, not helpful and I would not recommend him.

His teaching style is the worst; he does the calculation in his head or on paper that he does not show us, and tells us the final answer. He never tells how he got the answer until students ask him. His lectures are slow and without enthusiasm, so you start feeling sleepy in his class. His course policy is harsh. Only 1 drop for homeworks and lec.
I like his class! He offers a ton of material for you to review, and I love the concepts he presented. It's easy to fall behind, but stay on top of things.

He cares a lot about his students and adjusting the curriculum so it suits students' needs; his main goal is to make sure students leave his class well-equipped for future courses and the workforce, so students who are just there for the grade may not enjoy it. Although, his lecture style can be confusing sometimes.

I took his CS 141 course in the summer for 4 weeks, and even though it was loaded with a lot of assignments, he explained and taught how to code on the projector and answered any questions on the spot, he made the class humorous at times, and I personally found his cards and dice examples useful and it kept me engaged. Bro is goated.

(4 Week Summer Course) Class examples with cards and dice were useless. The class could've been more effective for students transitioning to C++ and should've had lessons coding it yourself in class instead of him coding and trying to explain. Work load is very heavy and often his explanations do not help much either. Would not recommend taking him
Shows a lot of empathy to his students. Gives good career advice and feedback.

Excellent educator. You will learn if you go to class. You will receive a good grade if you follow instructions. 211 is a challenging course, though utilize resources (including Koehler) and you will be successful. Exams are tough, but use Koehler/TAs to check answers. His RMP is outdated. He is one of the best UIC has to offer. TAKE HIS CLASS!

Now, CS251 has always been a hard class. It is one of the most important classes within the CS Dark Horse Trilogy (211, 251, 261), and I could not have been more glad to have taken it with Koehler. Heres my list of pros and cons ~
Pros
- Fast answers
- Great lectures and bonus videos
- great project selection
Cons
- tbh, can't think of one!
Decent guy, funny lecturer. Class was easy, no complaints.
Prof Koehler is a solid professor to take. His CS 211 class wasn't that bad. He is funny in lectures and from what I heard is a very nice person. Definitely don't hesitate to take him

Very rude and passive aggressive. He was basically a bully to students on online forums like piazza and answered questions in an insultingly rude manner at all times. I really despise this professor as he doesn't deserve to be called one and or to even teach at a highschool. I truly hope karma teaches him a lesson or two

Honestly, I do not know why he gets a bad rep for 251. Yes, the course was hard, and unless you're going into data science, tbh... mostly useless. However, as a professor he was not bad, he WANTS you to succeed, he gives a lot of resources, and explains most topics rather well in lectures. Labs were mostly about reinforcing lecture topics.

I am not taking CS 251. I am taking CS 211 this semester, and my friend and I finally decided to drop this course.

He is often aggressive towards students, which makes me feel extremely uncomfortable. I also heard about this from other students who took his classes before. I feel so bad seeing his replies to students...
Drop his class if you can. He is very aggressive to students.
DON'T take his class. Perhaps the top 2 worst instructors I have ever seen.

I was hesitant taking a class with Professor Koehler due to the reviews, but I am so glad I did. He expects a lot, but only cause he wants you to learn. The programs are challenging, but not impossible. He was very responsive on Piazza and his project descriptions and what he was expecting from you were very clear. My favorite thus far.

He wants you to succeed. The oral exams are LeetCode question interview style with a partner. The projects are not easy, but are manageable. He posts tons of resources on blackboard like code from class on his GitHub, and short video lectures on YouTube. His lectures are also good but make sure to pay attention and don't sit in the back.

I know some people don't like way he taught 251 when he started, but I don't think I could ask for a better professor or class structure. He set up the class to help students succeed in CS. Our oral exams are with partners and he is accessible through office hours. His lectures are great, but don't sit in the back.

SPRING 2023
---
PROS: He is accessible outside of class, and will willingly explain things to you. He grades fairly, and in my opinion generously gives out points. He only gives out oral exams, no written
---
CONS: He doesn't give out extra credit. He grades fair, but also strictly. 
---
SUMMARY:
He's underrated, and his RMP score is outdated IMO.

Can be a quite unorganized with the course, however that could be used to your advantage. He is very generous with grading and tries to help you out as much as possible. I did not attend lectures at all and still managed to get an A. Overall, just do your projects (on time) and start on them early cause most likely you'll need help.

he is a really good professor, the structure of the class is great and he is flexible with deadlines and he is willing to help you out. 
Great professor he takes feedback seriously and is trying to improve as a professor!!

I think half of the criticism on RMP for him has now become invalid since he did change a lot of his practices due to the backlash. He knows the material very well and is also flexible with deadlines in many cases. There is literally no written exam just 3 oral tests in a technical interview format which I think is a great way to test.
Often times very passive aggressive towards students.

He was very accessible outside of class and was able to elaborate really well on the questions in class. I feel like I really got a great understanding of data structures through his class and the conversations that I had with him outside of class. He had a lot of knowledge on the material and taught it well.

If he says project will be graded by xxx, don't believe him. He grades projects manually and from my experience, tries to get you as many points as you can get. Decent professor, but unacceptable start to the semester. Teaches moderately well. Projects are a lot of the grade, DONT fall behind. He is aggressive on Piazza at times. No issues with OH.

He was okay. He wasn't the best at the start, but he improved with time. Not very organized and late about release of projects and other important assignments/announcements. The projects are similar, but he doesn't provide no solutions and lectures don't really help. Office hours are packed and piazza responses are late and passive aggressive.

He is very knowledgeable professor one thing students don't like about him is that he grade projects manually which is a good thing, as your project will be graded on the accuracy and not on correctness

people dont like him because he had to hand grade hundreds of projects and it took him a while to do that. hes honestly a really chill and knowledgeable professor but the way students were verbally attacking him was out of control. hes one of the only cs profs ive had that actually responds to emails. if you need help in his class, just ask him.

if you take this professor, be expected to study everything on your own. This professor doesn't know anything outside of the slides that aren't his. The professor knows the teaching quality is low, so he gives extra credit if you can tolerate sitting in the class listening to someone reading slides.

people really hate on him, not sure why. no one attended class or started their programs early, so they like to complain. the dude was solid, fair, gave extensions in certain circumstances and admitted when he was wrong. ppl need to get real and stop expecting easy work

At first, I thought he was a fairly good professor, but I've come to realize after reflecting and numbering the number of good things you could take away from his class that he just isn't a good professor. His teaching is limited to his slideshows, if you ask him stuff outside of it he wont have a good response. He doesn't care about your situation

This Professor is the worst! Started the semester a little under the weather and messed up the first few weeks which we can't blame him for. But after that, extremely rude and tries to show that he is the Alpha Male and never responds to emails or piazza discussions. He reads off of the lecture slides and takes up 20/45 mins on class logistics.

Completely deaf to the valid criticisms students have had with his lack of actions on his promises to grade, assign work, and assistance on piazza. Despite paying attention and doing the best I can to get help on assignments, its been all for naught. His TAs are much more competent than him at actually getting students to understand the material.

idk why ppl are complaining so much,2 weeks for each project,where youre given half the code and clear steps for how to complete each milestone.drops a lot of hw,and has a reward system for evals. lectures are vague but with how much material needed to cover sorta makes sense. lots of easy points. he is a bit cold but can be very helpful at times

gave extensions for at least 2/6 projects,project description + jumpstart give you so much code/info its easy to start a project.first month was a mess,but now he responds to piazza posts on time.unlimited tries untimed on weekly quizzes,3 tries on mastery quizzes, all for which you can see your grade,easy points on labs and zybooks,easier than 141

Went almost every single class, struggled, and when I reached out for help on Piazza/office hours, it was radio silence.
He wastes half the lecture going over "logistics" that could easily be told over Piazza, and his explanations of topics were poor since he doesn't use his own lecture materials. 
If there is another professor, take them instead.

ugh I wish rate my professors had options like 'irresponsible', 'dont have no clue what they are doing', 'dont give a damn', or whatnot! That would be this Professor. Take his class only if you have no other choice and if you are ready to face it

Overall Koehler is a good professor. If you don't understand a topic you can go to office hours and he will help you. The only way to have a hard time or an issue with the class is if you don't apply yourself and go in expecting an easy A.
I'm planning to take this person for class next semester, I don't want to take it, but I have no other choice. Can UIC please change the professor to Pat Troy please!!!

Adam has given extensions on multiple project and is very responsive to messages. The complaints on here are coming from people who are unable to think for themselves. He knows the material very well and has proven to be someone I would take again. The guy is smart and people are doing themselves a disservice by not going to class.

Definitely not the best professor for CS. Made an in-person lecture online, and now I don't understand most of the things he teaches in class. No extensions were provided for the projects. Zero for not being a flexible professor. Knows how to make a challenging class even more difficult than it should be. Doesn't care about students' mental health.

The reviews make him seem like the worst teacher ever when in reality he's not. His communication/posting wasn't the best in the beginning but he did get a lot better and always extended deadlines. The way he teaches is also pretty good, always posts videos of what he goes over in class, and is easily digestible. Don't be afraid of the class!

he's about as good at teaching as a fourteen year old boy who forgot they had a project for a science fair due tomorrow, skimmed wikipedia, and created a google presentation written entirely in the comic sans font.
if you're expecting to actually learn anything, you're out of luck and might as well just learn everything from W3 Schools. sorry.

ONE OF THE BEST PROFESSORS EVER, HE DECIDES TO HAVE TWO IN PERSON CLASSES BUT FOR THE LAST ONE HE MAKES US GO ONLINE WHEN HES STILL ON CAMPUS, HE'S AN MURPHYS LAW FANATIC! HES GOT EVERYTHING UNDER CONTROL WOULD TAKE AGAIN IF I WANTED TO COMTEMPLATE QUITTING CS

The worst professor I have ever had. It took him 2 weeks to post syllabus. He could not even post a project on time even though it was the same as it was in the previous semester. I am really impressed with how bad and disorganized he is at teaching.
The professor is awesome. I'm taking him this year and he taught class while fighting in the Vietnam War. The dedication to teach through time is unreal. Full props for the hard work!

He is by far the worst professor I've ever had. Delayed important assignments for reasons he wouldn't explain, and then only gave a few days extension when he delayed the assignment for WEEKS. 

Takes AGES to respond to things on Piazza, and going to class is a waste of time. Better hope you don't have questions bc he's not going to answer them.

PLEASE AVOID AT ALL COSTS. He will just give excuses on why he is behind with material. I cant even learn about one of the most essential CS classes because of him (CS251). Almost a month in the semester and barely any material is posted.
I retook this class in the summer and he was pretty good!

this is 3 credit hour class and a 100 level class , but it felt like a 6 credit class. He gives way tooo much homework, projects every week, lab assignments every week, and  ILP every 3 weeks (basically you go one on one with a TA and they test you certain topics very nerve racking) I didnt even do this much in senior design lmfao

Terrible professor! This is an intro level course of CS and the projects he assigns expects one to know python in and out. Homework and labs makes one understand the language, but the projects assigned are insanely tough.

Professor Koehler was was very understanding but his class was difficult overall. This was my only coding class needed and it was hard to understand with no prior coding experience. If you don't pay attention to the lecture slides or homework you will not understand the labs which are worth majority of your grade.

Maybe smth happened last semester to warrant these reviews, but grading was timely this semester. Not harsh grading, the TAs were great, the course was pretty easy imo. I never watched the lectures (partially bc i didn't know they existed for a long time) and still got an A. I've only taken an intro course on python before, take that as you will

His CS109 class had a huge weekly workload and his video lectures are a little boring. I learned more from the TAs during the discussion but he did add a fair amount of extra credit for the class towards the end of the semester.
I like him

He is great I don't know why people don't like him, the class is fairly easy, and he gives plenty of help and there's only a lecture once a week, there is a good amount of hw but its all fairly simple and can be done in hour the only really involved parts ar the projects but you have a week to do them and only the 5th one is very difficult
Worst professor at UIC. Unresponsive, cold and discompasionate.

Fantastic Prof! He tries hard to be accessible and provide as many resources as possible to help students pass the class. It is a hard classhas weeded out its fair share of engineering majorsbut super applicable to future classes.

Class was completely online and was difficult to navigate through as you needed multiple accounts and subscriptions to complete homework and projects. Attendance is not required, class is overcomplicated and run by TA's. Unfortunately, if you are an engineering major you most likely will need to take this class and he is the only option (ATM).
This professor expects you to know everything while not teach anything. Do not take his class if you do not want to waste your money

Probably in the top 5 worst professors at UIC, taking this class gave me severe anxiety and depression. When I reacted out for help he practically told me too bad so sad. All these reviews are true, cs is a tricky subject but Koehler found a way to make it feel like rocket science:( AVOID AT ALL COST

All of these ratings are 100% accurate. Don't take this professor. Always coming late to lecture and only get about 15 minutes of actual lecture out of the whole 50 minutes. If you love being super confused, working on labs/projects for 10+ hours per week, and still learn nothing, you'll love this class. His class structure is ridiculous.

Do you like figuring things out by yourself? If you do, this professor is the one for you. The best thing about this professor is that he shows up 10-15 minutes late to every lecture. He is also nice enough to use up more than half of the lecture time to explain the due dates while using the rest of the time to complain about his students.

Prof. Kohler is the worst of the worst teachers. I have no words to describe the rudeness and lack of compassion towards students. He never opened matlab to show an example and had to wait hours for office hours. Withdrew the class because it shortened my life with two months, having a panic attack every other day. Avoid at all possible costs.
The structure of this class is so horrible that we're going into finals not having our midterm graded ... Professor's teaching is mediocre at best, but his class structure is utterly atrocious.

THE WORST FREAKING PROFESSOR I HAVE EVER WITNESSED IN MY WHOLE 19 YEARS OF LIVING ON THIS PLANET. I just took a final and the freaking midterm has still not even been graded. This is just straight up not only one of the biggest ironies that are characteristic of such lackadaisical and unprofessional "professors" but also is unethical!BEWARE HABIBIS

Always late to lecture. Very condescending and monotone. If you put in tons of time, google, YouTube, friends, office hours, etc you'll pass. But the lecture is trash. The help you receive will be very vague and confusing most times. Honestly avoid this professor if possible.

Koehler is one of the worst professors even though he was a TA for 10 years. I guess he never learned how to teach. He always starts his lectures late even though he is in the zoom meeting. His teaching style is nonexistent and relies on assigning labs and projects on a weekly basis to "teach" the class. Avoid him at all costs.

ONE OF THE WORST TEACHERS AT UIC. Horrible attitude and zero compassion towards students. Is 20 mins late to his own lectures and gets mad when students ask for help or questions. Also theres is no collaboration in the class at all so if you need help good luck! You leave his office hours more confused than before you went. Avoid at all cost!!!

New prof teaches cs 109, very un-organized and is constantly extending assignments because he forgets to post lecture videos, was excited for this class but the course felt like I was constantly having to teach myself everything. Forget about weekends because he assigns heavyy labs and projects those days, sadly has a bad attitude towards student .

Was struggling in the middle of the semester with depression and when I asked him for extra help/resources, he immediately told me to drop the class and count my losses because letting me complete missed assignments would have been "unfair" to the rest of the class since "everyone else has their own dilemmas".

Koehler is one of the worst professors at UIC. He is never on time to his own lectures, at least 10 minutes late, and he is a ridiculously hard grader. There is no compassion shown for students whatsoever. He rarely answers student questions and when he does it leaves you more confused than when you started. Avoid him at all costs.
